title: Adding Elements at a Specific Index in ArrayList

content.problemStatement:
In our previous lessons, we learned how to declare, initialize, access, and update elements in an ArrayList. Now, we'll learn how to add an element at a specific position using the `add(int index, E element)` method.

The `add(int index, E element)` method allows you to:
1. Insert an element at any valid position in the ArrayList
2. Shift all existing elements after that position to the right

---

Example:
```java
ArrayList fruits = new ArrayList<>();
fruits.add("Apple");    // ["Apple"]
fruits.add("Banana");   // ["Apple", "Banana"]
fruits.add(1, "Mango"); // ["Apple", "Mango", "Banana"]
```

- The index must be between 0 and the current size of the ArrayList (inclusive)
- Trying to add at an invalid index (like index 5 in a 3-element list) will throw an IndexOutOfBoundsException
- This is different from the simple add() method we learned earlier which always adds at the end
